tasks.title: Fix dependent job lookup to avoid LIKE false positives
evidence: internal/store/store_jobs.go:300-305 builds SQL pattern %"jobID"% and uses LIKE against depends_on JSON, which can match substrings (e.g., jobID="12" matches "123").
plan: Replace LIKE matching with an exact JSON match using json_each (SQLite) or equivalent JSON query for the store backend.
Add/extend store tests to cover substring cases (e.g., depends_on contains "123" should not match "12").
Verify by running `go test ./internal/store` and confirming the new test passes.
notes: Principles: Make bugs hard to write (E), Least astonishment (G).

tasks.title: Propagate context into AI extraction to allow cancellation/timeouts
evidence: internal/extract/extract.go:46-54 calls AIExtractor.Extract(context.Background(), ...) which ignores caller cancellation.
internal/crawl/worker.go:179-186 and internal/scrape/scrape.go:251-258 call extract.Execute without any context parameter to propagate timeouts.
plan: Add a context field/parameter to extract.Execute (or add ExecuteWithContext) and pass it through to AIExtractor.Extract.
Update scrape and crawl call sites to pass their request/job context into extraction.
Add extract tests with a stub AIExtractor that asserts context cancellation is respected.
Verify by running `go test ./internal/extract ./internal/scrape ./internal/crawl`.
notes: Principles: Fail fast (F), Operational hygiene (H), Make bugs hard to write (E).

tasks.title: Increase robots.txt scanner buffer to avoid false parse failures
evidence: internal/crawl/robots.go:172-177 creates bufio.Scanner without setting a larger buffer; scanner will error on lines >64K.
internal/crawl/robots.go:244 returns error on scanner.Err, causing fail-open behavior for oversized robots.txt files.
plan: Set scanner.Buffer with an explicit max token size (e.g., 1MB) before Scan() loop.
Add a robots parser test with a long directive line to ensure parsing succeeds and does not fail-open.
Verify by running `go test ./internal/crawl`.
notes: Principles: Fail fast (F) and Security baseline (I) due to compliance fail-open on parse errors.

tasks.title: Handle crypto/rand errors when generating webhook IDs
evidence: internal/webhook/dispatcher.go:38-41 calls rand.Read(b) without checking the error or read count.
plan: Update generateID to handle rand.Read errors (e.g., return error or use injectable reader with fallback).
Adjust call site to handle the new error path (skip record creation or log+fallback).
Add a unit test that injects a failing reader to assert error handling/fallback behavior.
Verify by running `go test ./internal/webhook`.
notes: Principles: Security baseline (I), Make bugs hard to write (E).

tasks.title: Harden e2e postJob helper with timeout and marshal error handling
evidence: internal/e2e/e2e_test.go:354-357 uses http.Post (no timeout) and ignores json.Marshal errors (data, _ := json.Marshal(body)).
plan: Replace http.Post with a shared http.Client that has a timeout (or pass client into postJob).
Handle json.Marshal errors by failing the test with t.Fatalf.
Update call sites to use the new helper signature.
Verify by running `go test ./internal/e2e -run TestE2E` (or the relevant e2e suite).

tasks.title: Sync .env with .env.example to restore config parity
evidence:
diff -u .env.example .env shows missing keys in .env, e.g.:
-REPLAY_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=30
-WEBHOOK_MAX_CONCURRENT=100
-WEBHOOK_ALLOW_INTERNAL=false
-AI_PROVIDER=...
-OLLAMA_URL=http://localhost:11434
plan: Add the missing keys to .env so it mirrors .env.example while keeping safe defaults.
Double-check comments to ensure alignment and avoid introducing real secrets.
Verify by running `diff -u .env.example .env` and confirming no unexpected deletions remain.
notes: Principles: DRY (C) and Least astonishment (G) for configuration sources of truth.

tasks.title: Document queue/redis/crawl-state env vars in .env.example
evidence: internal/config/config.go:134-144 defines QUEUE_BACKEND, REDIS_ADDR, REDIS_PASSWORD, REDIS_DB, REDIS_KEY_PREFIX, CRAWL_STATE_BACKEND.
plan: Add a dedicated section in .env.example documenting queue backend and Redis/crawl state variables with defaults.
Align comments with config defaults and valid values.
Verify by running `rg -n "QUEUE_BACKEND|REDIS_ADDR|REDIS_PASSWORD|REDIS_DB|REDIS_KEY_PREFIX|CRAWL_STATE_BACKEND" .env.example`.

tasks.title: Document exporter DB connection env vars in .env.example
plan: Add a database exporter section to .env.example documenting SPARTAN_POSTGRES_URL, SPARTAN_MYSQL_URL, and SPARTAN_MONGODB_URL.
Ensure comments clarify precedence vs. config-provided connection strings.
Verify by running `rg -n "SPARTAN_POSTGRES_URL|SPARTAN_MYSQL_URL|SPARTAN_MONGODB_URL" .env.example`.

tasks.title: Fix Biome lint warnings by using import type
evidence:
make ci -> biome lint warnings:
src/components/batches/BatchContainer.tsx:19:8 lint/style/useImportType
src/components/jobs/JobSubmissionContainer.tsx:19:8 lint/style/useImportType
plan: Update the two component files to use `import type` for type-only imports from ../../api.
Run `cd web && pnpm exec biome lint .` to confirm warnings are cleared.
Verify `make lint` remains clean.

tasks.title: Remove unused createRequestContext helper
evidence: rg -n "createRequestContext" internal/api shows only the definition in internal/api/ai_extract.go:149-150 (no references).
plan: Delete createRequestContext or wire it into handlers if intended.
Ensure any imports are cleaned up after removal.
Verify by running `rg -n "createRequestContext" internal/api` and `go test ./internal/api`.
